title: API Resources, Object Identity, Schemas, Desired State, and Status · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Object Uid Resourceversion
summary: A shared cluster rejects and defaults manifests differently across supported API versions while stale status leads a release team toward the wrong repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s object uid resourceversion increment in versioned API object contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use namespace, name, UID, resourceVersion, generation, timestamps, and managed fields to distinguish identity, concurrency, and history.
Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-object-uid-resourceversion — Use namespace, name, UID, resourceVersion, generation, timestamps, and managed fields to distinguish identity, concurrency, and history.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-api-objects-schema-status-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-api-objects-schema-status-4
    artifact: api-object.yaml
    decision: worked k8s api objects schema status 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 3
      status-evidence: observedGeneration-and-conditions
    verify: kubectl explain deployment.spec --api-version=apps/v1 && kubectl get deployment status-api -o json
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only evidence, stale status, unknown fields, or an API selected without feature-state and deprecation review.
    repair: Use live discovery and schema, separate spec from status, compare generation with observedGeneration, and preserve object UID and resourceVersion.
